message:
fix(search/chunk_vector_store): invalidate semantic embeddings when store metadata is missing or stale

`ensure_semantic_store_metadata` is responsible for noticing when the
on-disk semantic store no longer matches the runtime's expected
`semantic_schema_version` / model fingerprint and triggering a reset
before any embeddings are served. Two latent bugs were keeping that
guard from firing:

1. The freshness check used `stored.as_deref().is_some_and(|s| s != value)`
   to decide whether a `requires_reset` was needed. That predicate is
   false when `stored` is `None`, so a *missing* metadata row (which
   is exactly the "schema/model identity is unknown" case we want to
   treat as stale) silently passed the check and no reset was forced.
   Compare via `stored.as_deref() != Some(value.as_str())` so both
   "missing" and "mismatched" rows trip the reset.

2. The in-memory test helper (`open_in_memory`) opened the connection
   immutably and passed `&conn` to `ensure_semantic_store_metadata`,
   which takes `&mut Connection`. This compiled by accident only when
   features lined up a particular way; with the helper now used by the
   new regression test below, the borrow has to be `&mut`. Update the
   helper accordingly.

Add a regression test (`semantic_store_missing_metadata_invalidates_embeddings`)
that:

* opens a fresh store, seeds a chunk + vector, confirms semantic search
  returns the row,
* deletes the `semantic_schema_version` row directly via SQL to simulate
  the "metadata wiped or never written" failure mode,
* reopens the store, and asserts (a) semantic search returns no rows and
  (b) the affected generation is marked `Failed` — i.e. the reset path
  actually fired and the caller is forced to rebuild.
